name: asagynbaev/ZkpSharp
description: ZkpSharp is a .NET library for implementing Zero-Knowledge Proofs (ZKP). This library allows you to securely prove certain information (such as age or balance) without revealing the actual data. It uses cryptographic hashes and salts to ensure privacy and security.
proposal_draft:
Subject: Collaboration Proposal: Enhancing ZkpSharp with zk-SNARKs and PIVX SHIELD

Dear asagynbaev and the ZkpSharp contributors,

I have been following the development of ZkpSharp and am impressed by your focus on making Zero-Knowledge Proofs accessible within the .NET ecosystem. Your approach to leveraging cryptographic hashes and salts to verify attributes like age and balances without data exposure is an elegant solution for identity and data integrity. By abstracting these complex cryptographic concepts into a usable C# library, you are providing a vital tool for developers who prioritize user privacy in modern application architecture.

As the Technical Outreach Lead for PIVX, I see a significant opportunity to bridge your work with PIVX SHIELD, our privacy protocol based on zk-SNARKs (Zero-Knowledge Succinct Non-Interactive Arguments of Knowledge). While ZkpSharp excels at attribute verification, integrating the SHIELD framework—derived from the Sapling circuit—could allow your library to support industrial-grade anonymity sets and non-interactive private transactions. This would expand ZkpSharp’s utility from basic data verification to a full-featured privacy suite capable of handling shielded value transfers and more complex cryptographic primitives with high efficiency and minimal proof sizes.

We believe there is a strong synergy between your .NET implementation and our privacy-centric infrastructure. I would like to invite you to discuss a potential collaboration, which could range from a technical integration to a PIVX DAO development grant aimed at funding the expansion of ZkpSharp’s capabilities. Please let me know if you are open to a technical deep dive or a brief meeting to explore how we can mutually advance the standards of privacy-preserving technology.

Best regards,

[Your Name]
Technical Outreach Lead
PIVX DAO

name: brandonhimpfen/awesome-zero-knowledge-proofs
description: A curated list of awesome libraries, tools, frameworks, and resources for Zero-Knowledge Proofs (ZKPs) including zk-SNARKs, zk-STARKs, and other privacy-preserving cryptographic protocols.
proposal_draft:
Subject: Collaboration Proposal: Integrating PIVX SHIELD into Awesome Zero-Knowledge Proofs

Dear Maintainers of the brandonhimpfen/awesome-zero-knowledge-proofs repository,

Your curated list of zero-knowledge proof resources has become a vital touchstone for the cryptographic community, providing a comprehensive and structured taxonomy of SNARKs, STARKs, and privacy-preserving protocols. The depth of technical resources you have consolidated—spanning from foundational whitepapers to sophisticated developer frameworks—is impressive and reflects a clear commitment to advancing the accessibility of open-source privacy technology. As a project focused on the practical application of these primitives, we have found your repository to be an invaluable asset for both researchers and developers entering the space.

At PIVX, we have spent several years refining SHIELD, a privacy protocol built on the Sapling library that utilizes Groth16 zk-SNARKs to facilitate highly efficient shielded transactions. By leveraging these proofs, PIVX enables private peer-to-peer transfers that completely mask the sender, receiver, and transaction amount while maintaining high performance on a decentralized network. As one of the first Proof-of-Stake (PoS) blockchains to successfully implement zk-SNARKs, SHIELD serves as a robust, real-world reference implementation for developers interested in how zero-knowledge proofs can be integrated into high-throughput, consensus-driven environments without sacrificing privacy.

Given the alignment between your repository’s mission and our commitment to open-source privacy, I would like to propose including PIVX SHIELD as a technical reference or implementation example within your collection. Furthermore, as a community-governed DAO, PIVX frequently provides grants for research and development that benefit the broader privacy ecosystem; we would be eager to discuss how we might support your work or explore a deeper technical collaboration. Please let us know if you are open to a brief conversation regarding an integration or potential support through the PIVX DAO.

Best regards,

[Your Name]
Technical Outreach Lead
PIVX (Private Instant Verified Transactions)

name: Zyra-V21/ZKUzumaki
description: A privacy-focused Ethereum/SOL mixer using zero-knowledge proofs (ZK-SNARKs Groth16, SMT) for confidential transactions. Non Kyc, Non Signup.
proposal_draft:
Subject: Technical Collaboration Proposal: Enhancing ZKUzumaki Privacy with PIVX SHIELD

Dear Zyra-V21 Maintainers,

I have been closely following the development of ZKUzumaki and am highly impressed by your implementation of Groth16 zk-SNARKs and Sparse Merkle Trees to facilitate confidential transactions across Ethereum and Solana. Your commitment to a non-KYC, non-signup architecture aligns perfectly with the core ethos of the privacy-tech community. Specifically, the way you’ve optimized the proving circuits for your mixer to maintain high throughput while ensuring robust anonymity sets is a significant contribution to the EVM and Solana ecosystems.

As the Technical Outreach Lead for PIVX, I see a compelling synergy between your work and our SHIELD technology. PIVX was a pioneer in integrating Sapling-based zk-SNARKs into a Proof-of-Stake consensus model, providing a highly efficient and mature privacy protocol. By exploring an integration or a bridge with PIVX SHIELD, ZKUzumaki could benefit from our optimized UTXO-based shielded pool, potentially offering your users a deeper layer of liquidity and an additional gateway for privacy-preserving asset transfers. Our shared reliance on Groth16 constructions provides a common technical language that could streamline the interoperability of our respective privacy circuits.

We are interested in discussing how we might support the continued growth of ZKUzumaki, whether through technical collaboration on cross-chain privacy infrastructure or via a formal grant from the PIVX DAO. Our DAO frequently funds projects that expand the privacy landscape, and ZKUzumaki’s mission is a natural fit. Would you be open to a technical deep dive session next week to discuss how PIVX SHIELD can complement your current roadmap?

Best regards,

[Your Name]
Technical Outreach Lead, PIVX DAO

name: lidailin0730/CipherNebula
description: This repository is used to record some simulation - implemented solutions, mainly covering areas such as post - quantum cryptography, zero - knowledge proofs, and privacy - preserving protocols.
proposal_draft:
**Subject: Technical Collaboration Proposal: Integrating PIVX SHIELD with CipherNebula**

I have been closely following the developments within the `CipherNebula` repository and am thoroughly impressed by your rigorous approach to simulating complex cryptographic frameworks. Your work in implementing solutions for post-quantum cryptography and zero-knowledge proofs (ZKPs) provides a vital bridge between theoretical primitives and practical, scalable privacy-preserving protocols. The breadth of your simulations demonstrates a sophisticated understanding of the trade-offs required to maintain data integrity in decentralized environments.

As the technical outreach lead for PIVX, I see a significant opportunity for synergy between your research and our SHIELD technology. PIVX stands as a pioneer in the privacy space, being the first Proof-of-Stake project to successfully implement zk-SNARKs via our SHIELD protocol (based on a highly optimized version of Sapling). By leveraging zk-SNARKs, SHIELD enables near-instant, private transactions that ensure sender, receiver, and amount anonymity while maintaining a lean computational footprint. Integrating or benchmarking SHIELD’s implementation within your simulation environment could provide an excellent real-world case study on how zero-knowledge proofs can be effectively optimized for high-throughput privacy-preserving value transfers.

We would welcome the opportunity to discuss a potential collaboration or the integration of our codebase into your research simulations. Furthermore, because PIVX operates as a decentralized autonomous organization (DAO), we are in a position to offer development grants to support researchers who contribute to the advancement of privacy-preserving technologies. I would love to jump on a call or open a technical discussion on GitHub to explore how we might support your work or facilitate an integration that benefits the wider privacy community.

name: paritytech/bcts
description: TypeScript port of Blockchain Commons implementations: deterministic CBOR (dCBOR), Gordian Envelope for privacy-preserving data, Uniform Resources (UR), secret sharing (SSKR/Shamir), XID decentralized identities, provenance marks, LifeHash visual hashing, and FROST threshold signatures.
proposal_draft:
Subject: Collaborative opportunity: Integrating PIVX SHIELD with paritytech/bcts

Dear Parity Tech Maintainers,

I have been closely following the development of the `bcts` repository and am deeply impressed by your commitment to porting Blockchain Commons’ specifications to TypeScript. Your implementations of the Gordian Envelope for privacy-preserving data and the integration of FROST threshold signatures represent a critical advancement for the decentralized identity and air-gapped wallet ecosystems. By bringing these high-level cryptographic primitives—particularly dCBOR and SSKR—into the TypeScript environment, you are providing the essential building blocks for truly interoperable and sovereign digital infrastructure.

As the technical outreach lead for PIVX, I see a significant opportunity to complement your existing stack with PIVX SHIELD. SHIELD is a privacy protocol based on highly optimized zk-SNARKs (derived from the Sapling construction) that enables non-interactive, shielded transactions with near-instant finality. We believe that integrating zk-proof capabilities could provide a robust, private financial layer for your Gordian Envelope implementations. For instance, SHIELD could allow for the private settlement of value associated with XID decentralized identities or provenance marks, ensuring that while the data remains structured and secure via `bcts`, the transaction metadata and financial history remain entirely confidential.

We are interested in exploring a potential technical integration or supporting your team through a PIVX DAO grant to facilitate this work. Given our shared focus on privacy-preserving technology and sovereign data, we believe there is a strong alignment between our zk-SNARK implementation and your current work on threshold signatures and secret sharing. Would you be open to a brief technical sync to discuss how PIVX’s privacy features might enhance the `bcts` suite or to explore a collaborative development path?

Best regards,

[Your Name]
Technical Outreach Lead, PIVX DAO

name: 1200wd/bitcoinlib
description: The Python BitcoinLib provides developers with a wide range of tools to work with Bitcoin: manage wallets, private keys and addresses. Interact with the blockchain. And create complex transactions and scripts.
proposal_draft:
Subject: Collaboration Proposal: Integrating PIVX SHIELD (zk-SNARKs) with bitcoinlib

Dear 1200wd Maintainers,

I have been following the development of `bitcoinlib` for some time, and I am consistently impressed by the library’s comprehensive approach to simplifying complex blockchain operations. Your work in providing a robust, modular framework for multi-currency wallet management and transaction scripting has made high-level Bitcoin development significantly more accessible to the Python ecosystem. The project’s ability to handle everything from BIP-compliant key derivation to complex script construction makes it a foundational tool for developers building serious financial applications.

As the PIVX project continues to advance the state of decentralized privacy, we have successfully implemented SHIELD, a privacy protocol based on zk-SNARKs (specifically the Sapling construction). We believe that integrating SHIELD support into `bitcoinlib` would offer your users a powerful new primitive: the ability to construct zero-knowledge proofs for shielded transactions directly within your existing wallet framework. This would allow developers using your library to manage anonymized balances and execute private transactions with the same programmatic ease they currently enjoy for transparent UTXOs, effectively bridging the gap between Python-based development and cutting-edge privacy tech.

We are interested in exploring a potential collaboration to bring these privacy-preserving capabilities to the `bitcoinlib` ecosystem. This could take the form of a PIVX DAO-funded development grant to support the integration work or a closer technical partnership between our core contributors and your team. I would welcome the opportunity to discuss how we might align our roadmaps or provide the necessary technical documentation to facilitate this addition. Please let us know if you would be open to a brief technical sync to explore the possibilities.

Best regards,

[Your Name]
Technical Outreach Lead, PIVX DAO

name: gustavovalverde/zentity
description: Privacy-first identity verification built with zero-knowledge proofs, fully homomorphic encryption, and cryptographic commitments
proposal_draft:
Subject: Technical Collaboration Proposal: Enhancing Zentity Privacy with PIVX SHIELD

Dear Gustavo and the Zentity Maintainers,

I have been closely following the development of Zentity and am genuinely impressed by your sophisticated implementation of Zero-Knowledge Proofs (ZKPs) and Fully Homomorphic Encryption (FHE) to tackle the complexities of decentralized identity. Your approach to creating a privacy-first verification layer—specifically the use of cryptographic commitments to ensure data integrity without compromising user anonymity—is a significant step forward for the self-sovereign identity (SSI) ecosystem. The way Zentity balances verifiability with strict data minimization aligns perfectly with the core ethos of the privacy-tech community.

As the Technical Outreach Lead for PIVX, I see a compelling opportunity to augment Zentity’s identity framework with our SHIELD technology. PIVX SHIELD is a highly optimized privacy protocol based on zk-SNARKs (specifically the Sapling construction), designed for high-performance, shielded value transfers. By integrating SHIELD, Zentity could offer a seamless, private payment or incentivization layer that matches the anonymity set of your identity proofs. Utilizing our zk-SNARK implementation would allow Zentity users to execute transactions or settle verification fees with near-instant finality and total metadata obfuscation, ensuring that the financial trail does not deanonymize the identity layer you have worked so hard to protect.

We would welcome the opportunity to discuss a potential technical integration or the possibility of a PIVX DAO grant to support your development. Our treasury is dedicated to funding open-source privacy tools that complement our ecosystem, and Zentity’s infrastructure is an ideal candidate for such a partnership. Whether it is through shared research into ZK-circuit optimization or the implementation of a shielded payment gateway for your verification services, we are eager to explore how we can support your mission. Are you available for a brief technical sync next week to discuss these possibilities further?

Best regards,

[Your Name]
Technical Outreach Lead, PIVX DAO

name: bsc-digital-identity/digital-identity-system
description: The Digital Identity System is an open-source project developed by BSC Digital Identity. It provides a secure, privacy-preserving, and interoperable system for managing digital identities. The system is designed to support decentralized identity (DID) standards, enabling users to control their identity online.
proposal_draft:
Subject: Technical Collaboration Proposal: Enhancing Digital Identity with zk-SNARKs Privacy

Dear BSC Digital Identity Maintainers,

We have been closely following the development of the Digital Identity System and are impressed by your commitment to implementing W3C Decentralized Identifier (DID) standards with a focus on true interoperability. Your architectural approach to self-sovereign identity (SSI) provides a critical foundation for modern web privacy, particularly in the way your system empowers users to manage verifiable credentials without relying on centralized authorities. The balance you have achieved between cryptographic security and user-centric design sets a high benchmark for the decentralized identity space.

At PIVX, we believe there is a significant technical synergy between your identity framework and our SHIELD technology. By utilizing our advanced zk-SNARKs implementation—specifically based on the Sapling protocol—we can offer your system an additional layer of transactional and metadata privacy. Integrating SHIELD’s zero-knowledge proofs would allow your users to execute identity-linked actions or protocol-level payments while maintaining complete on-chain anonymity. This would ensure that the association between a user’s DID and their financial or activity metadata remains cryptographically shielded from third-party observation, further strengthening the privacy-preserving nature of your ecosystem.

We would welcome the opportunity to discuss a potential integration or how our cryptographic primitives might support your current roadmap. As a community-driven DAO, PIVX is also positioned to offer development grants for collaborative efforts that advance the state of decentralized privacy technology. Please let us know if you are open to a brief technical sync to explore how we might work together to enhance the privacy features of the Digital Identity System.

Best regards,

[Your Name]
Technical Outreach Lead, PIVX DAO
